explanation: This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle occurs (bool_exp1), then it will always be the case that either the robot will explain the situation (bool_exp3) and then will provide the reason for it (bool_exp2) or the robot will globally explain the situation (bool_exp3).

The property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, in the future, the robot should explain the off-normal condition or situation. The G operator guarantees that whenever the robot is in a state where an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the F operator guarantees that it must explain the off-normal situation at some point in the future.
This LTL property ensures that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, the robot provides an explanation of the unexpected obstacle, or the robot is in training mode.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) | (X ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))

explanation: The LTL property states that globally, if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then either the explanation for the obstacle is provided over a period, or eventually the explanation is provided.  This ensures the robot always explains any unexpected obstacles it encounters.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot should eventually explain the situation.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot should explain the situation (explain). The property ensures that the explanation happens immediately.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, the robot will explain the off-normal condition or will provide a reason.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle occurs (bool_exp1), and the robot is not currently following a path, the robot should explain the current path it is taking (bool_exp3) or be explaining its current path (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time steps.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot will eventually explain the situation. We use template 9 to ensure that the robot will explain the situation in the future.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered (unexpectedObstacle), then eventually (at some point in the future) the robot will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal). N_DURATION does not have any specific meaning in this case.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered and the robot is not already in a safe state (failSafe), then eventually (within N_DURATION time steps) it will either explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal) or the system enters a safe state (failSafe). This ensures that the robot responds appropriately to unexpected obstacles, either by providing an explanation or by prioritizing safety.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot will always explain the off-normal condition and how the situation is left in a safe state. The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true for all future states.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then globally, the robot must provide an explanation of the situation.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then the robot must eventually explain the off-normal condition.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, a detailed explanation of the off-normal condition should be provided.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, the robot will provide a detailed explanation about it, or the robot will always provide a detailed explanation. This ensures the robot will react and provide feedback in the face of the unexpected.

The LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the system should provide an explanation of the situation.  The LTL property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator, indicating this condition should always hold true. The boolean expression 'unexpectedObstacle'  is used.

This LTL formula states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then globally, the robot will make sure to provide an explanation about the unexpected obstacle situation.  This captures the essence of the prompt: explain if an unexpected obstacle is encountered.

This LTL property ensures that whenever an unexpected obstacle is encountered, and the robot is not already notifying users, then eventually the robot will notify users about the unexpected obstacle. The `G` (Globally) operator ensures this will always hold true.

The LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered (bool_exp1), and if the robot is also following a path, then eventually within a time window of N_DURATION, the robot's explanation should include its current path or, it will always explain the reason of the current unexpected behavior.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) | (X ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) V (bool_exp2 -> (((G[0,N_DURATION] (! bool_exp3)) | ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) V (! bool_exp3))) & ((F[0,N_DURATION+1] bool_exp3) | (F[0,N_DURATION] (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))))))))))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) V (bool_exp2 -> (((G[0,N_DURATION] (! bool_exp3)) | ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) V (! bool_exp3))) & ((F[0,N_DURATION+1] bool_exp3) | (F[0,N_DURATION] (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))))))))))

explanation: This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then the system should provide an explanation of the unexpected obstacle.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, or always within a time window (specified by N_DURATION), the robot must provide an explanation.  The use of 'explain' from the atomic propositions is directly related to 'explain' in the natural language, indicating the robot's obligation to explain the obstacle.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle occurs, then it will always explain the situation. The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator, ensuring this explanation happens at all future time steps.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered (bool_exp1), then it is always the case that the robot will explain the off-normal condition (bool_exp3) in the future.  N_DURATION is set to 5, it doesn't have any impact on the meaning of the LTL, because of the usage of the Global operator G.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually the robot will explain the off-normal condition or the situation is in a safe state. The `G` operator ensures this holds at all times.  `bool_exp1` captures the condition of an unexpected obstacle, `bool_exp2` sets the antecedent that triggers the explanation, `bool_exp3` and `bool_exp4` define the desired outcome

This LTL formula states that if an unexpected obstacle occurs, then eventually, the robot will provide an explanation of the situation.  The formula uses the Globally operator to ensure that this property holds true at any time. The F (Finally) operator ensures that eventually an explanation is provided.

This LTL formula states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot will always explain the unexpected obstacle. This directly addresses the prompt by ensuring an explanation is always provided upon the specified event.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then in every future state, the system will explain something (it is up to the system to decide what should be explained after an unexpected obstacle is encountered).
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the system will always provide an explanation for this occurrence.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered (bool_exp1), then it will eventually explain why that obstacle exists. This captures the core request of explaining the unexpected obstacle.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually the robot will explain the off-normal condition and also the robot will provide an explanation.  We use template 199 as it captures the condition-action style behavior. When unexpected obstacle occurs it is guaranteed that some of the explanation-related actions will eventually occur.
chosen_LTL_template: ((((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) U bool_exp3))) | bool_exp1)

explanation: This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then it must explain the off-normal condition within a certain time frame.  Specifically, the `G` (Globally) operator ensures that this explanation always occurs. The `bool_exp1` is simply the unexpectedObstacle.  The `bool_exp3` is then used in the (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)) pattern.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle occurs, it will always be followed by an explanation of the situation.  The property will always hold true.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, the robot will provide a reason. This ensures that the robot explains why an obstacle was encountered. The 'G' operator ensures this holds globally, at all times.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot should always explain the situation.

The LTL property states that globally, if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then globally it will be followed by an explanation of the situation. N_DURATION is set to 5, defining the time period where this must happen.

The LTL property states that globally, if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then globally, it must be the case that the robot should explain the situation. The LTL template used here is a basic implication within a global context, ensuring the explanation always follows the obstacle.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot will eventually explain the off-normal condition and provide a detailed explanation.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) | (X (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> (((G[0,N_DURATION] (! bool_exp3)) | (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (! bool_exp3))) & ((F[0,N_DURATION+1] bool_exp3) | (F[0,N_DURATION] ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))))))) & ((! bool_exp1) -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> (((G[0,N_DURATION] (! bool_exp3)) | (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (! bool_exp3))) & ((F[0,N_DURATION+1] bool_exp3) | (F[0,N_DURATION] ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))))))

explanation: The LTL formula states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually the robot explains the off-normal condition. This ensures the robot provides an explanation for encountering the obstacle. N_DURATION is a positive integer which determines the time bound in the formula.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, the robot will provide an explanation. The `G` operator ensures this holds true at all times. The `F` operator specifies that something will happen eventually.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then the robot provides an explanation for it, but only after time has passed, as G is used with the help of N_DURATION.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually the robot will explain the off-normal condition, which is a direct response to the input's request for an explanation when an unexpected obstacle is encountered. This is ensured by the `G` (Globally) operator, implying that this relationship holds true throughout the mission. N_DURATION determines the maximum time step within which the explanation will occur.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually the robot will explain the off-normal condition or explain the current path.  This captures the requirement that upon encountering an obstacle, the robot must provide some form of explanation.
This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, the robot will eventually explain the off-normal condition and provide the reason for it for a fixed amount of time.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered (unexpectedObstacle) and the robot is not in a state where it has successfully completed its mission (missioncomplete), then the robot must provide an explanation (explain) regarding the obstacle.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered (bool_exp1), then eventually, within a certain time frame (defined by N_DURATION), the robot will explain the off-normal condition (bool_exp3). The LTL template `G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))` enforces this condition.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, the robot will explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal), or if not, the robot will continually explain the off-normal condition (explainOffNormal).  The use of 'V' (weak until) implies that the robot must eventually explain the unexpected obstacle, though this explanation can happen at any point after the obstacle is encountered, or in the case where no explanation ever happens (the robot is always providing it).

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then eventually, either the robot provides its current location or it provides information about the path it is currently taking, or both. The "V" operator ensures that at least one of these actions happens. The use of 'G' ensures this holds true from the point where the obstacle is encountered onward.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then it will always provide an explanation for that obstacle. This is captured by the chosen template, which implies that any time the condition 'unexpectedObstacle' holds, the explanation is guaranteed to happen.

This LTL property states that if an unexpected obstacle is encountered, then the robot must eventually explain why this obstacle was encountered and the robot will continue to explain why the obstacle was encountered throughout the mission or until the mission is complete. The chosen template ensures that after encountering an unexpected obstacle, the robot will always provide an explanation.
